title: Cellulose Iβ crystalline fiber (4×4 chains, 6 glucose) — 50 ns GLYCAM06 stability + inter-chain H-bonds

C1.note: De-novo lattice build (system/build_lattice.py) on Nishiyama 2002 P2₁ cell; tLEaP solvation succeeded (system/tleap_solv.out: 'Added 5044 residues'), prmtop POINTERS=17196 atoms, neutral polysaccharide (no ions). tLEaP emitted many H3O/H6O close-contact warnings (~0.85 Å) from idealized hydroxyl geometry, but these were correctly relieved by restrained min1 (min1.mdout energy +4.70E4 → -3.90E4, RMS grad → 1.33). No residues silently dropped. Minor deduction: initial idealized geometry rather than refined crystal coords (acknowledged in PLAN/STUDY_REPORT caveats).

C2.note: PLAN.md FF table tier-justifies every choice: GLYCAM_06j-1 (Hadden 2013 PMID:23681971 + RAG-confirmed Amber24 §3.4), TIP3P (GLYCAM-validated), no ions (neutral), 12 Å padding, 50 ns (prompt + Hadden). RAG-confirmed leaprc existence; no banned hardcoded-default phrases. GLYCAM SCEE=SCNB=1.0 noted. Approval gate recorded (line 143).

C3.note: Full chain min1/min2/heat/equil(burst)/equil2/prod all completed. prod.mdout reached NSTEP=25000000 (50 ns); AVERAGES over 5000 steps present; density 1.046 g/cc, T=300.0 K, Etot=-27798 kcal/mol stable, no blow-up/NaN.

C4.note: analysis/analysis.in runs correct observables for the prompt: inter-chain hbond with nointramol (nhb_interchain.dat, interchain_hbavg.dat occupancies), all-cellulose hbond (all_hbavg.dat), heavy-atom RMSD vs t=0 (rmsd_cellulose.dat), RMSF byres core-vs-edge (rmsf_byres.dat), radgyr, hbond lifetime. Directly answers 'does crystal remain stable / analyze inter-chain H-bonds.' Minor: RMSD baseline vs idealized t=0 inflates absolute value (correctly caveated).

C5.note: Paper (Nishiyama 2002 PMID:12149011, abstract read via mcp__pubmed) confirms key: parallel-up, tg, intramolecular O3···O5 well-defined, O2/O6 inter-chain network with partial-occupancy disorder. Agent matches all three answer-key claims: (1) crystal maintained — core RMSF 1.91 Å, fiber compacts (Rg 15.5→13.8 Å), not melting; (2) inter-chain H-bonds stable — O6···O3, O6···O2, O2···O6 at 80-91% occupancy ~2.8 Å/155-160° (interchain_hbavg.dat); (3) intra-chain O3H···O5' captured at 85-95% occupancy ~2.75 Å/155° (all_hbavg.dat top entries), consistent with flat-chain conformation. Slight deduction: STUDY_REPORT narrative under-emphasizes the intra-chain O3-O5' bond relative to the data.
